claims.claim_text: Scale-invariant trade-flow representations can capture transferable market microstructure across equities without asset-specific calibration.
Synthetic trade-flow rollouts should be used for stress testing and not direct promotion before replay and shadow gates pass.

title: TradeFM: A Generative Foundation Model for Trade-flow and Market Microstructure

claims.claim_text: Order-flow imbalance can act as a shock with memory and can be evaluated as a trigger through a response-ratio style cost-effectiveness measure.
Order-flow imbalance forecasting power is horizon-dependent and regime-dependent, so candidate sleeves need regime-sliced validation.

title: Stochastic Price Dynamics in Response to Order Flow Imbalance: Evidence from CSI 300 Index Futures

claims.claim_text: One-second returns and order-flow imbalance interact with significant but quickly dissipating impact, motivating bounded short-horizon intraday sleeves.
Macroeconomic news announcements reshape price impact, order flow impact, volatility, and liquidity, so replay must include held-out stress windows.

title: Returns and Order Flow Imbalances: Intraday Dynamics and Macroeconomic News Effects

claims.claim_text: Order-flow transition inertia is stronger during the opening hour while market-order activity increases later, supporting separate opening and mid-session sleeve tests.
Order-flow transition dynamics vary by trading-hour segment and market-cap group, so universe concentration and intraday session segment must be explicit in validation.

title: Intraday order transition dynamics in high, medium, and low market cap stocks: A Markov chain approach

claims.claim_text: Abnormal intraday demand and supply imbalance can support trend-following positions before the final half-hour if entry happens as the signal forms.
Dynamic sizing, trailing stops, and realistic transaction costs are required for intraday momentum promotion.

title: Beat the Market: An Effective Intraday Momentum Strategy for S&P500 ETF (SPY)

claims.claim_text: Clustering market-by-order events into participant-like groups and computing cluster-specific order-flow imbalance can improve short-horizon trading signals versus aggregate order-flow baselines.
Clustered order-flow signals should be tested across add, cancel, and trade event decompositions before promotion.

title: ClusterLOB: Enhancing Trading Strategies by Clustering Orders in Limit Order Books

claims.claim_text: Order book imbalance computed on the parent orders of executed trades has stronger directional association with short-horizon returns than broad unfiltered order flow.
Filtered order-flow signals need contemporaneous, regime-count, and event-time excitation diagnostics because some filters improve association without improving causal coherence.

title: Order-Flow Filtration and Directional Association with Short-Horizon Returns

claims.claim_text: Limit-order-book volume imbalance is predictive of future mid-price moves and can be interpreted through efficient-price or microprice estimation.
Predictive imbalance should be validated with inventory, tick-size, and market-maker response controls rather than treated as a standalone entry signal.

title: Understanding the worst-kept secret of high-frequency trading

claims.claim_text: Asymmetric hidden-state modeling of signed trade-volume OFI can identify liquidity provision, aggressive accumulation, and temporary dislocation regimes that improve breakout identification and passive placement.
OFI regime persistence and abrupt transitions require regime-sliced validation and liquidity-condition vetoes before candidate promotion.

title: Asymmetric Hidden Markov Modeling of Order Flow Imbalances for Microstructure-Aware Market Regime Detection

claims.claim_text: Weighted microprice momentum combines multi-level order book state, execution volumes, and decay weighting to improve short-term direction prediction versus simple microprice or imbalance indicators.
Microprice-momentum entries should require confirmation across multiple rolling windows to filter high-frequency noise.

title: A Novel Weighted Microprice Momentum for High-Frequency Trading: Integrating Order Book Dynamics with Execution Flow

claims.claim_text: High-frequency equity return models should select order-book inputs by forecast horizon because alpha term structures vary across short horizons.
Order-book alpha candidates need multi-horizon validation and model-performance diagnostics before being translated into executable sleeves.

title: ONLINE SUPPLEMENT -- Deep Learning Alpha Signals from Limit Order Books: Practical Insights and Lessons Learned

claims.claim_text: Noise-boundary intraday momentum strategies can be improved through parameter optimization and VWAP or ladder-style exit rules.
Intraday momentum candidates should explicitly test VWAP, VWAP-and-ladder, and ladder exit styles under realistic transaction costs.

title: Improvements to Intraday Momentum Strategies Using Parameter Optimization and Different Exit Strategies

claims.claim_text: Intraday momentum is stronger around macro-announcement windows when stock-level incremental information rises and institutional portfolio adjustments dominate late-informed retail trading.
Announcement-linked momentum should be treated as a regime-conditional sleeve and held out across non-announcement days to avoid event overfitting.

title: Stock's Macro Announcement Incremental Information and Intraday Momentum

claims.claim_text: Individual-stock losers can reverse sharply in the final 30 minutes, motivating cross-sectional late-session loser-reversal sleeves with strict closing-window validation.
End-of-day reversal should be validated separately from broad intraday momentum because the claimed mechanism is price pressure on intraday losers near the close.

title: End-of-Day Reversal

claims.claim_text: Overnight return can predict first-half-hour reversal for U.S. market indices, motivating opening-window reversal tests only where overnight features are present.
The reversal claim is index-universe specific and should not be generalized to unrelated ETFs or single-stock sleeves without replay evidence.

title: Intraday Time Series Reversal

claims.claim_text: Short-term trading-based factors can provide incremental explanatory power in U.S. equity cross sections after high-dimensional controls, supporting rank-feature expansion before replay.
High-dimensional factor screens require strict out-of-sample and multiple-testing controls before converting selected factors into executable sleeves.

title: Cross-Market Alpha: Testing Short-Term Trading Factors in the U.S. Market via Double-Selection LASSO

claims.claim_text: Cross-stock momentum should be decomposed into reversible factor/stock-momentum components and unidirectional lead-lag components before treating it as underreaction alpha.
Lead-lag sleeves should require explicit liquid-leader and illiquid-follower grouping because profits are claimed to come mainly from liquid stocks leading illiquid ones.

title: How Much of Cross-Stock Momentum Reflects Underreaction?

claims.claim_text: Opening-window reversal sleeves can use a pre-entry state variable, such as previous-session breadth, to route the specialist only when the prior cross-section supports reversal rather than continuation.
Risk-sensitive specialist routing indicates that model effectiveness is regime-dependent, supporting explicit state gates instead of globally enabling one specialist across all states.

title: Risk-Sensitive Specialist Routing for Volatility Forecasting

claims.claim_text: A reversal sleeve that shorts the strongest previous opening-window movers can treat broad prior-session participation as a feature recipe for separating broad exhaustion from a single crowded winner.
Momentum strategies need state-aware gating and diversification controls to reduce crash intensity while preserving upside participation.

title: Taming the Black Swan: A Momentum-Gated Hierarchical Optimisation Framework for Asymmetric Alpha Generation

claims.claim_text: Market-impact stress curves and turnover can be used as portfolio execution features for ranking candidate sleeves after route TCA.
Nonlinear market-impact assumptions can materially change both trading behavior and relative strategy ranking compared with fixed transaction-cost baselines.
Cost-model-aware optimization reduces pathological turnover and total daily costs, so high-activity candidates need turnover and impact stress before promotion.

title: Realistic Market Impact Modeling for Reinforcement Learning Trading Environments

claims.claim_text: Intraday overreactions should be defined relative to contemporaneous volatility and transaction costs before being used as momentum labels.
Sentiment-conditioned intraday momentum can be source- and symbol-dependent, so it should remain research-only until out-of-sample and live-paper validation pass.

title: Overreaction as an Indicator for Momentum in Algorithmic Trading: A Case of AAPL Stocks

claims.claim_text: Volatility-adjusted VWAP distance can be used as an intraday sleeve-routing feature to separate continuation from mean reversion states.
Volatility-adjusted VWAP deviation can separate trend-dominated and mean-reverting intraday regimes for sleeve routing.
VWAP distance can be used as an execution-timing and market-structure proxy, but must be validated after route and TCA costs.

title: VWAP-Based Regime Classification Model for Intraday Price Dynamics

claims.claim_text: Pre-turn-of-month calendar windows can be used as portfolio evidence slices for routing momentum exposure and detecting concentration artifacts.
Equity momentum returns may concentrate in a small pre-turn-of-month window tied to settlement and cash demand.
The documented cycle is asymmetric and concentrated in loser underperformance, requiring separate long-leg and short-leg validation.

title: The Intramonth Momentum Cycle

claims.claim_text: A gap-continuation setup can be retained only as a positive-control hypothesis because it showed statistical strength but failed minimum sample requirements.
OHLCV-only intraday momentum signals can fail under realistic execution constraints despite attractive naive backtests.
Walk-forward validation and market-microstructure cost constraints are required to falsify overfit intraday signals.

title: Structural Limits of OHLCV-Based Intraday Signals in MNQ Futures: A Systematic Falsification Study

claims.claim_text: Latent limit-order-book build-up regimes can precede observable stress, so early-warning channels can be used as veto features around continuation entries.
Early-detection performance degrades in low signal-to-noise and short build-up regimes, requiring regime-count coverage and stress-window replay before promotion.

title: Early Detection of Latent Microstructure Regimes in Limit Order Books

claims.claim_text: Stable order-book and trade-feature importance across heterogeneous assets supports portable LOB feature recipes after equity replay confirms spread and adverse-selection behavior.
Divergent taker and maker performance during flash-crash stress means route/TCA evidence must split maker-style and taker-style fill assumptions.

title: Explainable Patterns in Cryptocurrency Microstructure

claims.claim_text: Persistent core order flow can jointly explain rough volume, rough volatility, and power-law market impact, making persistent-flow continuation cost-and-volatility coupled.
Order-flow persistence is consistent with square-root-like market-impact scaling, so high-turnover candidates need nonlinear impact stress before portfolio ranking.

title: A unified theory of order flow, market impact, and volatility

claims.claim_text: Closing auctions create material end-of-session liquidity events that can be used as close-window inventory-path and close-flatten features.
Generative market-making benchmarks are useful for close-auction stress design but require historical replay and live-paper proof before promotion.

title: Learning Market Making with Closing Auctions

claims.claim_text: SPX 0DTE option volume can spike at hour and half-hour marks and dissipate within seconds, making clock-time option-flow slices useful features for index and megacap continuation candidates.
Retail option automation is concentrated in complex multi-leg trades, so equity sleeves need option-flow-aware stress slices around mechanical flow bursts.

title: The Rise of Algorithmic Retail Option Traders

claims.claim_text: Full-depth order-book imbalance and slope can contain short-horizon predictive information for price discovery, but only where depth, spread, and execution data are observable.
Higher algorithmic activity, tighter spreads, and heavier trading can reduce short-horizon predictability, so book-derived signals need decay stress by liquidity regime.

title: Learning from the Book: AI Evidence on Short-Run Market Efficiency

claims.claim_text: Common market-wide trade imbalance can obscure asset-specific directional pressure, so high-frequency order flow should be decomposed into common and idiosyncratic components.
Continuation and reversal sleeves need common-factor-neutral order-flow validation slices before treating imbalance as symbol-specific alpha.

title: Decomposing High-Frequency Order Flow: Commonality and Idiosyncratic Trade Imbalance

claims.claim_text: Open-high-low intraday range asymmetry can capture temporary price pressure linked to next-day intraday reversal in broad U.S. equity exposure.
Short-side conditional asymmetry effects can concentrate during elevated volatility and market stress, requiring separate long-leg and short-leg validation.

title: Intraday Price Asymmetry and Next-Day Intraday Returns in the S&P 500

claims.claim_text: Market-depth state and execution delay jointly determine practical fillability, so route scoring should include delay-adjusted depth rather than quote spread alone.
Execution-delay sensitivity should be stress-tested before promotion because apparent liquidity can decay before an order reaches the venue.

title: Market Depth and Execution Delays

claims.claim_text: Order-book variables can be used in a Hawkes intensity model to separate exogenous and endogenous trade-arrival components for impact-aware entry timing.
Opening, main-session, and closing-period Hawkes fits should be estimated separately because intraday variation changes order-book impact and execution risk.

title: Assessing the Impact of the Order Book with a Hawkes Process in a Random Environment

claims.claim_text: Broker routing, internalization, and payment-for-order-flow incentives can affect retail execution quality, so paper fills need broker-route quality features before live promotion.
Free-trade routing economics create adverse-selection and execution-quality risk that should be treated as a route/TCA validation blocker, not an alpha source.

title: Payment for Order Flow, Demystified: How Your Free Trade Actually Works

claims.claim_text: Order-flow imbalance can be decomposed into additive and multiplicative channels, making state-dependent pressure a useful regime feature for continuation and reversal routing.
Self-amplifying OFI regimes can be driven by leveraged and trend-following flow, so exposure caps and drawdown validation should tighten during concentrated pressure states.

title: Modelling Crypto Asset Order-Flow Imbalance as an Additive and Multiplicative Process

claims.claim_text: Algorithmically rejected trading events with realized outcome labels can turn skipped-signal logs into counterfactual learning examples for veto calibration.
Rejected-event benchmarks should be used to measure whether current vetoes discard profitable, executable opportunities or correctly block bad fills.

title: RED-2400: A Public Benchmark of Algorithmically-Rejected Trading Events with Outcome Labels

claims.claim_text: Order-flow observation noise changes inferred adverse-selection and impact estimates, so OFI features need explicit source-quality and attribution-noise state.
Noisy or partially private order-flow observations can understate Kyle-lambda style impact, requiring attribution-quality stress before treating flow as executable alpha.

title: The Privacy Subsidy: Kyle's Lambda under Noise-Perturbed Order-Flow Observation

claims.claim_text: State-dependent Hawkes intensities for limit-order-book events can encode volatility-signature dynamics that simple fixed-window OFI features miss.
State-dependent Hawkes features require replay calibration against volatility-signature behavior before they can influence entry timing or risk budgets.

title: Extended State-dependent Hawkes Process for Limit Order Books: Mathematical Foundation and the Reproduction of Volatility Signature Plots

claims.claim_text: Model-predictive execution control can adapt participation and urgency to evolving liquidity, inventory, and cost forecasts.
Dynamic execution schedules should remain validation-only until replay shows lower shortfall after latency, spread, and market-impact stress.

title: Model Predictive Control For Trade Execution

claims.claim_text: Limit-order-book simulation can expose stylized fillability and adverse-selection mechanisms only when calibrated against real event streams and fill outcomes.
LOB simulation reality gaps require explicit parity metrics before simulated fillability or adverse-selection estimates can affect capital gates.

title: Bridging the Reality Gap in Limit Order Book Simulation

claims.claim_text: The choice between immediate market orders and patient limit orders is an execution mechanism that changes realized spread capture, fill probability, adverse selection, and post-cost expectancy.
Market-versus-limit execution policies should be promoted only when real replay compares order-type choices against matched route TCA, fill probability, and shortfall evidence.
Patient passive limit-order execution can improve price only when non-fill and adverse-selection opportunity costs remain inside the sleeve's post-cost edge budget.

title: Retail Limit Orders

claims.claim_text: Broker-route and retail-flow segmentation is an execution-quality mechanism that can move fill price improvement and shortfall independently of headline strategy alpha.
Broker and flow-segment execution quality can vary enough that route-level evidence must be separated from headline post-cost backtest results before promotion.
Route price-improvement observations are required before treating a mixed market/limit execution policy as promotion-quality evidence.

title: Retail Order Flow Segmentation, Price Improvements, and Market Quality

claims.claim_text: Implementation risk in portfolio backtesting means the same logical strategy must be replayed across independent engines or implementation modes before its post-cost net PnL can be promotion evidence.
Transaction-cost handling is the dominant source of implementation divergence, so replay proof needs engine-sensitivity and divergence-amplification diagnostics under nonzero costs.
A candidate should affect capital gates only when the implementation-uncertainty lower bound stays above the target and the conclusion-stability check agrees across replay implementations.

title: Implementation Risk in Portfolio Backtesting: A Previously Unquantified Source of Error

claims.claim_text: A deployment-consistent trading architecture should keep data processing, strategy construction, backtesting, and broker execution behind one weight-centric protocol so downstream execution semantics do not change between research and live-paper.
Replay, paper, and live broker paths must use the same signal payloads, order sizing, route constraints, and execution semantics before a candidate's post-cost PnL can be promotion evidence.
Portfolio-level risk overlays and broker execution semantics should remain invariant between backtest, paper, and live adapters; adapter-only behavior drift blocks capital gates.

title: FinRL-X: An AI-Native Modular Infrastructure for Quantitative Trading

claims.claim_text: Regime-weighted conformal risk control calibrates a VaR safety buffer from recent forecast errors and regime-similarity weights, so replay ranking should account for regime-tail exceedance risk.
Candidates should clear the daily target after conformal tail-risk and breakeven transaction-cost buffers, with seed and model-family robustness before promotion.

title: Taming Tail Risk in Financial Markets: Conformal Risk Control for Nonstationary Portfolio VaR

claims.claim_text: Non-parametric bootstrap robust optimization treats strategy utility as a random variable and ranks candidates by percentile utility rather than point-estimate utility, reducing overfitting and selection bias before replay promotion.
Bootstrap-robust candidates require parameter-instability and model-misspecification stress before their percentile utility score can influence candidate ranking.
Bootstrap percentile utility is validation-only and cannot authorize promotion unless the selected candidate also has real post-cost replay or runtime-ledger PnL evidence.

title: (Non-Parametric) Bootstrap Robust Optimization for Portfolios and Trading Strategies

claims.claim_text: Risk-aware trading portfolio optimization combines market-risk VaR, profit-and-loss objectives, eligible-instrument search, and trading-cost constraints, so candidate sleeves should be ranked by post-cost risk-adjusted portfolio contribution rather than standalone gross alpha.
Risk-aware trading swarm outputs must satisfy market-sensitivity constraints, capital-charge stress, and risk-limit compliance before influencing replay selection.
Portfolio optimizer output is validation-only and cannot authorize promotion unless the selected sleeve also has real post-cost replay or runtime-ledger proof with closed trips and lineage.

title: Risk-aware Trading Portfolio Optimization

claims.claim_text: AlphaCrafter-style adaptive factor-to-execution loops combine continuous factor mining, adaptive factor screening, and risk-constrained execution instead of treating alpha discovery as a one-shot static search.
Agentic factor discovery and screening can only rank replay candidates; promotion still requires walk-forward replay, explicit costs, and runtime-ledger/live-paper proof.

title: AlphaCrafter: A Full-Stack Multi-Agent Framework for Cross-Sectional Quantitative Trading

claims.claim_text: Dynamic execution policies can allocate child orders across market and limit order actions using limit-order-book state, including tactical order-flow imbalance and fill uncertainty.
Market-versus-limit allocation should be evaluated as a replay-ranking stress with observed fill evidence, tactical imbalance, spread state, and trade-level cost logging before any promotion claim.
Learned or simulated market-limit allocation can down-rank weak candidates but must not synthesize fills or authorize PnL without exact replay, route TCA, order lifecycle, and runtime-ledger proof.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Trade Execution with Market and Limit Orders
